title: requests — cross-module relations (no local tables)
description: This module owns NO tables and therefore has NO anchors. Every relation documented here is either (a) a READ this module performs against another module's table or (b) a WRITE this module orchestrates on another module's table via that module's public API. The `AccessRequest` DTO is a PROJECTION across these tables — it is not itself an Eloquent model. Consumers who want a single object should reach for the DTO, not join manually.

usedFor: Every access-request read is a projection over this table filtered by `action_key='access.grants.create'`.
The `AccessRequestPresenter` binding hydrates the underlying instance + its `context_json` + its resolved requirements when producing an `AccessRequest` DTO.

queryHotPaths.sql: SELECT * FROM approval_instances WHERE action_key = 'access.grants.create' AND tenant_id = :tenant AND requester_id = :caller ORDER BY requested_at DESC
queryHotPaths.index_needed: workflow/approvals should carry an index on (action_key, tenant_id, requester_id, requested_at DESC) — tracked as a dependency in workflow/approvals' relations.json

queryHotPaths.purpose: list requests I'm an approver for (`GET /api/v1/access-requests/pending-my-review`)
queryHotPaths.sql: SELECT ai.* FROM approval_instances ai JOIN approval_requirements ar ON ar.instance_id = ai.id WHERE ai.action_key = 'access.grants.create' AND ai.status = 'pending' AND ar.resolved_approvers::jsonb ? :caller_id ORDER BY ai.requested_at DESC

usedFor: Resolving the approvers list for the AccessRequest projection.
The `pending-my-review` endpoint filters on `resolved_approvers @> [caller_id]` (Postgres JSONB containment).
The DTO surfaces the count + summary of approve/reject decisions to the requester so they can see who decided what.
Rejection reason is projected from the most recent `approval_decisions.comment` where `decision='reject'` on the failing requirement.

When status='approved', the DTO projects the resulting access_grants row (LEFT JOIN by `source='access_request' AND source_reference=<instance_id>`) so a single API call returns both the request outcome AND the grant that was materialised. Zero rows on the LEFT JOIN when the grant was later revoked.

The `permission_belongs_to_resource_type` validation rule cross-checks that each `permissions_wanted` string exists on the target resource type's declared permission set (from `#[AccessRequestable(permissions: [...])]`) AND is a valid permission name in `permissions` for the sanctum guard + caller's application.
Requester's active tenant is derived from the `X-Application-Id` + Sanctum PAT stack. The `resource_in_caller_tenant` rule confirms `<resource>.tenant_id === caller.active_tenant_id`.
Requester + approver + resource-owner projections. All three are `User` rows.

writesTo.via: The `approve` middleware creates the `approval_instance` in response to the dispatched `CreateAccessRequestAction`. This module never writes to approval_instances directly — it dispatches the action and lets the middleware do the write. This preserves the whole approval-engine invariant chain (context snapshot, template resolution, requirements graph, timer registration).

`AccessRequestGrantMaterializer` calls `GrantIssuer::issue()` from `access/grants`. Never manipulates `access_grants` directly. Ensures the grant carries `source='access_request'` + `source_reference=<approval_instance_id>` for traceability (queryable both ways).

referencesTo.description: Approval instances created by this module carry cross-module references in their context_json. This shape is a CONTRACT between this module and workflow/approvals — changing it requires a coordinated migration.

context_json_shape_for_access_grants_cre.resource_owner_id: string|null — resource.created_by, for fallback approver resolution
context_json_shape_for_access_grants_cre.permissions_wanted: string[] — subset of the resource's declared permission set
context_json_shape_for_access_grants_cre.requester_role_names: string[] — snapshot at submit time, informs rule evaluation
context_json_shape_for_access_grants_cre.requester_tenant_id: string — the tenant we're operating in
context_json_shape_for_access_grants_cre.approver_summary: object — resolved at approval time; contains first_approver_id + total_approvers_count
context_json_shape_for_access_grants_cre.materialised_grant_id: string|null — written back after the grant is created; enables round-trip lookup

notes: This module INTENTIONALLY has no `x-eloquent` model contract. Consumers who want to interact with an access request programmatically should use the `AccessRequestPresenter` binding to hydrate a DTO, not touch approval_instances directly.

Cross-tenant references are forbidden — the `resource_in_caller_tenant` rule catches attempts. Because approval_instances are already scoped by `tenant_id`, this constraint is enforced at BOTH the write path (validation rule) AND the read path (BelongsToTenant global scope on the workflow/approvals model).

The `materialised_grant_id` writeback into `approval_instances.context_json` after `AccessRequestGrantIssued` is the only mutation this module performs on workflow/approvals' owned rows. It goes through `workflow/approvals::AppendInstanceContextAction` — never a raw update.
